note: Mathlib/SetTheory/ZFC/Basic.lean:44 def ZFSet : Type (u+1) := Quotient PSet.setoid; module docstring literally states '# A model of ZFC', modeling Zermelo-Fraenkel (+choice) set theory inside Lean's type theory (extensionality, empty set, pairing, union, power set, infinity, foundation, replacement/separation, choice all proved as ZFSet theorems).
verify_note: ZFSet exists (Mathlib/SetTheory/ZFC/Basic.lean def, confirmed via decl_exists/oracle) but per Mathlib's own module docstring it is 'A model of ZFC' -- a type/model, not the axiomatic theory itself; Wikidata Q191849 is classed as an 'axiomatic formal system' (the theory). The brain graph already links ZFSet to the distinct-but-related Q191853 'Zermelo set theory' via a softer 'generalization' bond, not 'formalizes'/exact -- claiming 'exact' here overclaims a model-of-theory relation as identity; 'related' would be honest.

note: Mathlib/Order/Monotone/Defs.lean:62 def Monotone (f : α → β) : Prop := ∀ ⦃a b⦄, a ≤ b → f a ≤ f b -- the standard order-theoretic definition of a monotonic (order-preserving) function.
verify_note: Monotone exists (Mathlib/Order/Monotone/Defs.lean, confirmed via decl_exists/oracle) but the file's own module docstring explicitly warns: 'Contrary to standard mathematical usage, monotone/mono here means increasing, not increasing or decreasing.' Wikidata Q194404 defines 'monotonic function' as a function that 'preserves or reverses' order -- i.e. both directions -- while Mathlib's Monotone covers only the increasing half (Antitone is the decreasing half). 'exact' overclaims; this is at best 'related'/'partial'.
